disclosure: The scene is an authored, schematic teaching reconstruction. Imported and procedural assets support atmosphere and navigation; none count as historical evidence or document the exact appearance, ownership, location, or scale of Varennes in June 1791.

assets.title: Grounded district architecture, lighting, lanterns, and road surface

historicalBasis.rationale: The geometry establishes a legible late-evening teaching space without claiming an exact site plan or architectural survey.

uses.purpose: Create a cohesive late-evening district around the deterministic four-zone teaching layout.

limitations.location: Building, road, and lantern placement is authored for traversal rather than historical geography.
limitations.ownership: No facade, furnishing, lantern, or environmental object is assigned to a historical owner.
limitations.scale: Building dimensions, road width, spacing, and sightlines are compressed and not to scale.
limitations.appearance: Materials and silhouettes are grounded stylization, not a verified reconstruction of Varennes in June 1791.

assets.title: Evidence stations, disclosures, and navigation props

historicalBasis.rationale: These reconstructed prop forms provide interaction affordances; canonical evidence remains in the DOM records they open.

uses.purpose: Render navigation markers and non-evidentiary source stations separately from dramatized cast figures.

limitations.location: Station placement follows gameplay transforms, not a documented historical arrangement.
limitations.ownership: Furniture and display forms do not establish historical ownership.
limitations.scale: Prop dimensions are adjusted for classroom readability and collision safety.
limitations.appearance: Procedural station props are reconstructed interface markers, not authentic artifacts.

assets.title: Generic procedural investigator, historical-role markers, and ambient residents

historicalBasis.rationale: Generic procedural bodies and clothing palettes distinguish gameplay roles but do not reproduce the appearance of Drouet, Louis XVI, the investigator, or any resident.

uses.purpose: Render dramatized, non-evidentiary cast markers with shared procedural geometry and profile-aware motion.

limitations.location: Figure placement and movement follow gameplay transforms, not a documented historical arrangement.
limitations.ownership: Clothing colors and silhouettes do not establish identity, rank, or ownership beyond the interface label.
limitations.scale: Figure dimensions and motion are adjusted for readability, collision safety, and graphics profiles.
limitations.appearance: Every figure is a generic dramatized role marker, not a portrait, likeness, or verified reconstruction of clothing or body shape.

assets.title: Procedural dusk wind and distant tone

historicalBasis.rationale: The optional soundscape is an atmospheric accessibility-controlled layer and makes no historical sound claim.

uses.purpose: Provide optional low-volume ambience after explicit player activation.

limitations.location: The generated ambience is not localized to a documented street or building.
limitations.ownership: No sound is attributed to a historical person, object, animal, or institution.
limitations.scale: Volume, frequency, and mix are tuned for interface comfort rather than acoustic simulation.
limitations.appearance: The soundscape is a dramatized mood layer and not evidence of what Varennes sounded like.

assets.title: Guided pursuit and reduced-motion repair reconstruction

historicalBasis.rationale: The route trace, checkpoints, figures, markers, and reduced-motion sequence visualize reducer-owned repair steps without claiming exact geography, speed, timing, or historical appearance.

uses.purpose: Present the bounded repair sequence through equivalent spatial and reduced-motion interfaces.

limitations.location: The repair corridor, route trace, checkpoint positions, and action-marker placement are authored teaching geometry rather than historical geography.
limitations.ownership: Figures, markers, controls, and path elements do not establish a historical actor's ownership or exclusive agency.
limitations.scale: Distances, speeds, lateral movement, spacing, and timing are compressed and not to scale.
limitations.appearance: The spatial and reduced-motion presentations are schematic reconstructions, not evidence of roads, people, vehicles, clothing, or objects in June 1791.

historicalBasis.rationale: A generic vintage table supports interaction staging but does not document a Varennes artifact.

uses.purpose: Provide a consistent grounded base for evidence and archive interaction surfaces.

limitations.location: Table placement is invented for navigation and evidence access.
limitations.ownership: The model is not presented as property of Drouet, Louis XVI, or any resident of Varennes.
limitations.scale: Scale is adjusted to the interaction and camera system rather than measured from a historical artifact.
limitations.appearance: The generic vintage design is not evidence of the furniture used during the royal journey.

license.optionalCredit: Wooden Table 01 by Ethan Place via Poly Haven.

steps: Embedded the original glTF, binary mesh, and 1K textures into GLB.
Resized textures to a 512-pixel maximum, converted them to WebP, and applied Meshopt compression with glTF Transform 4.4.1.
Kept authored primitive colliders independent from the visual mesh.

historicalBasis.rationale: Generic weathered barrels add scale and material variation but do not document goods or containers at Varennes.

uses.purpose: Non-interactive set dressing near the post road.

limitations.location: Barrel clusters are positioned for composition and never establish a historical storage site.
limitations.ownership: No historical person or institution is identified as an owner.
limitations.scale: Cluster size and spacing are adjusted for the compressed teaching district.
limitations.appearance: The generic maritime and farmhouse reference is not proof of local barrel design in 1791.

license.optionalCredit: Wooden Barrels 01 by James Ray Cock via Poly Haven.

steps: Embedded the original glTF, binary mesh, and 1K textures into GLB.
Resized textures to a 512-pixel maximum and converted them to WebP.
Simplified meshes to a 0.65 target ratio and applied Meshopt compression with glTF Transform 4.4.1.
Kept the cluster non-interactive and outside evidence and progression systems.

historicalBasis.rationale: A generic worn paving material supports grounded readability but does not establish the surface used on the historical route.

uses.purpose: Surface the compressed continuous teaching road.

limitations.location: Material placement follows the authored route rather than a documented paving survey.
limitations.ownership: The surface implies no historical owner or responsible institution.
limitations.scale: Texture repetition and road dimensions are selected for rendering and are not to scale.
limitations.appearance: The generic mossy cobble reference does not document the actual road material or condition in June 1791.

license.optionalCredit: Cobblestone Floor 08 by Rob Tuytel via Poly Haven.

steps: Selected and renamed the 1K diffuse, OpenGL normal, and roughness JPG maps.
Applied reduced normal intensity at runtime and retained source resolution for acceptable close-range road detail.

historicalBasis.rationale: Poly Haven establishes the asset's creator, rights, and technical file graph, not the appearance or placement of plaster in Varennes in June 1791.

uses.purpose: Apply a non-evidentiary generic plaster material to authored modular facade wall surfaces in High and Balanced presentation profiles.

limitations.location: Runtime application to authored plaster facade surfaces is a compositional choice, not documentation of a Varennes building, facade, or position.
limitations.ownership: No surface is attributed to a historical person, household, business, or institution.
limitations.scale: The source's 2 m texture scale and bounded 1-to-8 quarter-step runtime UV repeats are rendering choices, not measurements of a historical facade.
limitations.appearance: Generic weathered surface only; not proof of Varennes plaster formula, paint color, age, damage, ownership, location, or facade.

license.optionalCredit: Painted Plaster Wall by Amal Kumar via Poly Haven.

steps: Copied the three original 1K JPG files byte-for-byte to stable runtime paths without renaming, resizing, re-encoding, recompression, color-space conversion, channel packing, or any other content modification.
At runtime, clone the diffuse, normal, and roughness maps, use repeat wrapping, and apply bounded scale-aware UV repeats derived from authored facade dimensions.
At runtime, apply an authored facade color tint, a 0.32 normal scale, and a 0.94 roughness constant; these editorial transformations do not modify the static source files.
Treat all apparent historical fit as editorial inference; the source and license records establish rights and technical origin only.

historicalBasis.rationale: Poly Haven establishes the asset's creators, rights, and technical file graph, not the appearance or placement of masonry in Varennes in June 1791.

uses.purpose: Apply a non-evidentiary generic masonry material to authored modular facade wall and footing surfaces in High and Balanced presentation profiles.

limitations.location: Runtime use on authored masonry facade surfaces is placement for composition, not documentation of a Varennes structure or position.
limitations.ownership: No stone, wall, quarry, building, or repair is attributed to a historical owner or institution.
limitations.scale: The source's 2 m texture scale and bounded 1-to-8 quarter-step runtime UV repeats are rendering choices, not measurements of historical masonry.
limitations.appearance: The material cannot establish exact stone species, quarry, mortar, coursing, construction date, facade, or condition in Varennes.

license.optionalCredit: Stone Wall by Charlotte Baglioni and Dario Barresi via Poly Haven.

steps: Copied the three original 1K JPG files byte-for-byte to stable runtime paths without renaming, resizing, re-encoding, recompression, color-space conversion, channel packing, or any other content modification.
At runtime, clone the diffuse, normal, and roughness maps, use repeat wrapping, and apply bounded scale-aware UV repeats derived from authored facade dimensions.
At runtime, apply an authored facade color tint, a 0.46 normal scale, and a 0.98 roughness constant; these editorial transformations do not modify the static source files.
Treat all apparent historical fit as editorial inference; the source and license records establish rights and technical origin only.

historicalBasis.rationale: Poly Haven establishes the asset's creator, rights, and technical file graph, not the appearance, construction, or placement of timber in Varennes in June 1791.

uses.purpose: Apply a non-evidentiary generic timber material to authored timber-front wall surfaces in High and Balanced presentation profiles.

limitations.location: Runtime orientation and placement on authored timber-front surfaces are compositional choices, not documentation of a Varennes timber element or position.
limitations.ownership: No beam, door, shutter, board, cart, sign, or structure is attributed to a historical owner or institution.
limitations.scale: The source's approximate 1.5 m texture scale and bounded 1-to-8 quarter-step runtime UV direction and repeats are rendering choices, not historical measurements.
limitations.appearance: The material does not prove wood species, board width, fasteners, joinery, vertical orientation, wear, or placement.

license.optionalCredit: Wood Planks by Amal Kumar via Poly Haven.

steps: Copied the three original 1K JPG files byte-for-byte to stable runtime paths without renaming, resizing, re-encoding, recompression, color-space conversion, channel packing, or any other content modification.
At runtime, clone the diffuse, normal, and roughness maps, use repeat wrapping, and apply bounded scale-aware UV repeats derived from authored facade dimensions.
At runtime, apply an authored facade color tint, a 0.38 normal scale, and a 0.90 roughness constant; these editorial transformations do not modify the static source files.
Treat all apparent historical fit as editorial inference; the source and license records establish rights and technical origin only.

historicalBasis.rationale: Poly Haven establishes the asset's creator, rights, and technical file graph, not the roof material or appearance of any Varennes building in June 1791.

uses.purpose: Apply a non-evidentiary generic clay-roof material to authored modular roof panels in High and Balanced presentation profiles.

limitations.location: Runtime application to authored roof panels is compositional and cannot identify a specific Varennes building or roofline.
limitations.ownership: No tile, roof, building, household, business, or institution is assigned a historical owner.
limitations.scale: The source's 4 m texture scale, bounded 1-to-8 quarter-step runtime UV repeats, and authored roof pitch are rendering choices, not historical measurements.
limitations.appearance: The material does not establish that a specific Varennes building used clay tiles, or establish tile profile, color, course, age, pitch, damage, or exact roofline.

license.optionalCredit: Clay Roof Tiles by Amal Kumar via Poly Haven.

steps: Copied the three original 1K JPG files byte-for-byte to stable runtime paths without renaming, resizing, re-encoding, recompression, color-space conversion, channel packing, or any other content modification.
At runtime, clone the diffuse, normal, and roughness maps, use repeat wrapping, and apply bounded scale-aware UV repeats derived from authored roof dimensions.
At runtime, apply an authored roof color tint, a 0.42 normal scale, and a 0.92 roughness constant; these editorial transformations do not modify the static source files.
Treat all apparent historical fit as editorial inference; the source and license records establish rights and technical origin only.

historicalBasis.rationale: Poly Haven establishes the asset's creators, rights, and technical file graph, not the composition, condition, ownership, or placement of metal in Varennes in June 1791.

uses.purpose: Retain a ledgered but unused metal candidate; it is not imported or applied by the current runtime.

limitations.location: No current runtime surface uses this material; any later hardware application would require a new reviewed placement.
limitations.ownership: No fitting, tool, vehicle, building, person, business, or institution is assigned historical ownership.
limitations.scale: The source's approximate 2.2 m texture scale is metadata only because the current runtime applies no UV repeat or metallic constant from this family.
limitations.appearance: The material cannot establish alloy, forging, hardware form, paint, corrosion age, owner, or exact location, and must not imply that every visible fitting was heavily rusted.

license.optionalCredit: Rust Coarse 01 by Dimitrios Savva and Rico Cilliers via Poly Haven.

steps: Copied the three original 1K JPG files byte-for-byte to stable runtime paths without renaming, resizing, re-encoding, recompression, color-space conversion, channel packing, or any other content modification.
Not imported or applied by the current runtime; no tint, repeat, normal-scale, roughness, or metallic editorial transform is active.

historicalBasis.rationale: Poly Haven establishes the HDRI's creators, rights, and technical origin, not weather, sky color, visibility, or atmospheric conditions at Varennes in June 1791.

uses.purpose: Provide High and Balanced environmental lighting and a dusk background without loading in Classroom.

limitations.location: The panoramic sky is not a photograph or observation from the archive or Varennes.
limitations.ownership: No sky, horizon, weather condition, or illumination is attributed to a historical observer or institution.
limitations.scale: The panorama has no evidentiary spatial or distance scale in the teaching district.
limitations.appearance: Dusk color, cloud cover, exposure, rotation, and intensity are editorial presentation choices, not historical evidence.

license.optionalCredit: Qwantani Dusk 2 Pure Sky by Greg Zaal and Jarod Guest via Poly Haven.

steps: Copied the original 1K HDR file byte-for-byte to a stable runtime path without re-encoding or recompression.
High and Balanced use the panorama for environmental lighting and background presentation; Classroom does not load it.
Treat all apparent historical fit as editorial inference; rotation, intensity, exposure, sky color, and cloud cover are not historical claims.
